message:
fix(web): reduce scroll flicker with longer scroll-end timeout

PROBLEM:
Mouse wheel scrolling caused visible flickering due to the 150ms scroll-end
timeout. With discrete wheel ticks (~100-200ms apart), the timeout would fire
between ticks, triggering:
1. isScrolling = false, full redraw WITH post-processing
2. Next wheel tick: isScrolling = true, full redraw WITHOUT post-processing
This alternation between post-processed and non-post-processed frames created
visible flickering.

SOLUTION:
- Increase scroll-end timeout from 150ms to 400ms
- This keeps isScrolling = true through typical wheel event gaps
- Post-processing now only applies after actual scroll stops

ADDITIONAL FIXES:
- Add dedicated layoutRetryTimer (fix timer type confusion)
- Add fx-enabled/fx-disabled classes for perf mode
- Disable backdrop-filter and animations when fx-disabled
- Fix dashboard-layout from display:contents to display:block
